title: This life-hunting rover may be SpaceX's 1st-ever Mars launch
summary: SpaceX will launch Europe's Rosalind Franklin Mars rover in 2028, but not on its Starship rocket. This marks SpaceX's first Mars mission involving a life-hunting rover.

title: Watch live: Blue Origin reusing New Glenn rocket stage for 1st time on April 19 launch
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket is set to launch on April 19 with a reused first stage for the first time. The event will be livestreamed for public viewing.

title: New moon of April 2026 brings incredible views of the constellation Hydra, Jupiter, Venus and more this week
summary: The new moon in April 2026 will offer excellent visibility of constellations like Hydra, planets including Jupiter and Venus, and faint galaxies in the dawn sky. This astronomical event provides a prime opportunity for stargazing.

title: 'Tall waves moving in slow motion': Here's how oily oceans on Saturn's giant moon Titan may behave
summary: Scientists study wave behavior on Saturn's moon Titan, where oily oceans exhibit slow-motion waves influenced by liquid properties and low gravity. The research highlights how wave dynamics differ from Earth's oceans.

title: 'For All Mankind' alternative timeline vs reality: How Apple TV's sci-fi show diverges from history
summary: Apple TV's sci-fi show 'For All Mankind' imagines an alternate timeline of space exploration where the Soviet Union lands on the Moon first, diverging significantly from real historical events. The series explores how this pivotal change reshapes six decades of space history.

title: Hubble Space Telescope spots a mesmerizing spiral galaxy | Space photo of the day for April 17, 2026
summary: The Hubble Space Telescope has captured a stunning image of a spiral galaxy, showcasing its ongoing contributions to astronomical discoveries. This latest photo highlights the telescope's enduring legacy in space exploration.

title: Northern lights could be visible as far south as Illinois and Oregon tonight
summary: A fast solar wind is expected to trigger geomagnetic storms tonight, potentially making the northern lights visible as far south as Illinois and Oregon. This event could push auroras into mid-latitudes across the U.S.

title: Artemis 2's heat shield seems to have aced its trial by fire
summary: Artemis 2's Orion capsule's heat shield successfully endured its re-entry trial through Earth's atmosphere. Mission commander Reid Wiseman confirmed the heat shield performed exceptionally well.

title: Interstellar invader comet 3I/ATLAS made a startling transformation as it passed the sun
summary: The interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS underwent a significant transformation after passing the sun in fall 2023, according to astronomers' observations.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires Following the successful return of Orion, attention for the Artemis program has now shif

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew on the International Space Station conducted vehicle departures and a spacewalk in March. The activities were highlighted as part of a busy month for the mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool mission concluding its CRS NG-23 supply run.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marked a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration efforts.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China introduced new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making refinements at Starbase Pad 2 in preparation for the return of Booster 19. The updates are part of ongoing efforts to enhance launch infrastructure at the facility.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II successfully conducted a lunar flyby, setting a new record. The Orion spacecraft, carrying four astronauts, completed the mission as part of NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station (ISS), while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. This comes as NASA's Artemis II mission nears its midpoint.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This publication commemorates the agency's significant milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026. The event includes an online preparatory forum in April-May and an in-person forum in Paris on June 8-9, 2026.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location where the signal was received is described as ideal for navigation purposes.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improvements in attitude control systems. It highlights a breakthrough or innovation in this field.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team used ESA's parabolic flight campaign to test graphene aerogels propelled by lasers in microgravity, achieving instant acceleration. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, suggests potential applications for steering solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Lasers and graphene could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated this potential.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space-based computing. This advancement could significantly enhance the capabilities of satellites and spacecraft.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Thruster Market with Gauss
summary: Rocket Lab is expanding into the thruster market with its new Gauss line, aiming to address scalability concerns for customers. The company's CFO highlighted the need for vertical integration to ensure reliable supply at scale.

title: Axiom Space’s Suit Set to Fly in 2027
summary: Axiom Space is preparing its first qualification spacesuit for a 2027 flight, with production ongoing despite external developments. The company's chief astronaut confirmed the suit's progress in an interview with Payload.

title: Voyager Technologies Wins Its First Private ISS Mission
summary: Voyager Technologies has secured its first private mission to the International Space Station (ISS), marking a new entrant in the commercial spaceflight competition. Axiom Space previously held a monopoly on NASA's private ISS missions, but new providers like Voyager Technologies are emerging for the 2026 opportunities.

title: Seraphim Space Aims To Shape Strategy With New Advisory Council
summary: Seraphim Space has formed a new advisory council to address key challenges in the space industry, including sustainability, regulations, national security, and international collaboration.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: ORBX ETF launches to track companies earning over half their revenue from space tech, including transportation, software, and satellite services. This follows a wave of space tech IPOs.

title: Kepler Leads Team to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test its 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The initiative aims to demonstrate advanced space-based data transmission capabilities.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget concerns, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He highlights the passion and scientific potential of the space industry while questioning the financial planning behind major projects.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: CLD companies challenge NASA's statements, while NASA invites them to disprove the claims. The dispute was highlighted during the Space Symposium.

title: Slingshot Introduces AI-Powered Portal Platform
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched an AI-powered portal platform to help operators detect anomalies and make decisions efficiently. The platform serves as a centralized hub for space operations.

title: ICON Prime Launches, Led By Former TX Rep
summary: ICON Prime, led by a former Texas representative, has launched as a new business unit focused on collaborating with the Pentagon and NASA. The robotic construction company ICON aims to expand its operations in space-related projects through this initiative.

title: IFC News Roundup from AIX
summary: The AIX Aircraft Interiors Expo highlighted satellite companies advocating for in-flight connectivity (IFC) solutions to airlines. Via Satellite reported key announcements in this sector.

title: Airlines See IFC as Way to Build Relationships With Customers
summary: Airlines are leveraging in-flight connectivity (IFC) to enhance customer relationships and improve the flying experience. The trend highlights the growing importance of connectivity in aviation.

title: NorthStar Earth & Space to Go Public in SPAC Deal at $300M Valuation
summary: NorthStar Earth & Space plans to go public via a SPAC deal valued at $300 million. The Canadian space domain awareness company integrates ground-based systems for its operations.

title: ESA Taps Kepler to Lead Next Stage of HydRON Optical Program
summary: The European Space Agency has selected Kepler Communications as the prime contractor for HydRON Element 3, the next phase of a sovereign optical communications constellation. This marks a significant step in advancing optical communication technology in space.

title: Space Force, NATO Commanders Share Threat Perspectives
summary: U.S. Space Force is actively identifying new space capabilities and managing threats from adversaries while creating missions to defend assets. NATO commanders share perspectives on space threats during a discussion in Colorado Springs.

title: Air Force Awarded Development Contract for Space-Based AMTI; Meink Says Risk Low
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force awarded a contract to multiple vendors for the development of space-based air moving target indication (AMTI) systems. Officials, including Meink, indicated low risk in the project's execution.

title: Boeing and Millennium Space Systems Add Mid-Class Resolute Satellite Bus
summary: Boeing and its subsidiary Millennium Space Systems have introduced a new mid-class satellite platform called Resolute, offering enhanced capabilities compared to traditional small satellites. The platform aims to provide increased performance for various space missions.

title: Slingshot Aerospace Releases AI-Powered Analytics Platform for Space Operations
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched Portal, an AI-powered analytics platform for satellite data monitoring and analysis. The software integrates Slingshot’s data flows with external sources to enhance space operations.

title: Industry Builds the Case Around Architecture Options for Orbital Data Centers
summary: The space industry is exploring orbital data centers as a viable alternative to terrestrial ones, driven by lower satellite launch costs and increasing demand. This emerging architecture aims to provide new solutions for data storage and processing in space.

title: Transport Layer of PWSA Will Eventually Transition From SDA to New Acquisition Executive, Sandhoo Says
summary: The Space Force plans to transfer the communications layer of the SDA's PWSA to a new acquisition executive. This transition is part of the Space Force's updated acquisition structure.
